feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned users from IAS to SAC.

However, when I change a user in IAS (e.g., updating the last name) and run the Read job, the changes are not provisioned to the target system (SAC).

There is no error, but the users are read, and no update is performed in the target system.

What should I pay attention to and what needs to be configured to ensure that only the user changes are successfully provisioned?

 

Many Thanks

Best regard

entry.title: Best Practices for Managing Users and Groups in Azure and IAS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Currently, we have the following scenario implemented: We have Azure as the IdP → IAS as a proxy → Cloud Apps. The groups are created in Azure, and users are assigned to those groups in Azure. These groups with their users are then provisioned to SAP IAS. From SAP IAS, the users/groups, such as SAC_Group, are provisioned to the SAC app, or the group ENABLE_Group is provisioned to Enable Now.

Since we, as the SAP team, do not have access to the Azure tenant, I am asking if there is a better approach or any best practices for managing users/groups.

Could we provision only users from Azure and create the groups directly in IAS, then manually assign users to these groups und then provision to Cloud Apps? Are there any disadvantages to this approach?

What are your experiences? How do you handle this in your environment? What are the best practices: Should users/groups be managed in Azure, or should groups be managed directly in IAS?

Which approach is better: Managing users/groups in Azure or provisioning users to IAS and managing the groups there?

Thank you so much!

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS Deletes Business User Group in SAP
entry.content:
When Syncing our Users from the IAS to SAP, the Business User Group of our Users is set empty.

We don't want IAS to do Group Governance within SAP.

I have already removed everything from the transformations, that included Group.

Why is it still deleting User Groups set in SAP?

THX!!!

entry.title: Successfactors initial deployment - Identity provisioning really needed?
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are initially deplyoment a 3 environment SF landscape. This came pre-bundled with one IAS DEV Tenant and re-used two other existing IAS tenants. So our setup is as such

  • IAS DEV = SF DEV
  • IAS QAS = SF QAS + IBP QAS
  • IAS PRD = SF PRD + IBP PRD

Now our implementation partner advises on provisioning of users from SF to IAS.

Is it really needed to have users synced from SF to IAS? I see it seems mandatory to some extend when a use case is People Analytics. However, there ought to be also some old school Sync using OData which functions directly between SF and PA?

Any clarficiation or confirmation would be nice.

Jens

entry.title: IPS - transformation - adding a user to a group using patch
entry.content:
Hi all.
In out IAS tenant users are created by replication from SF.
Now we have requirement to update nickname and groups for some users. It should be done by replication from ABAP system.
Base on docs https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/patched-and-merged-attributes?q=contains  we have prepared a patch transformation. 
It works if only nickName is updated 

 

 

{
    "user": {
        "mappings": [
            {
                "sourcePath": "$",
                "targetPath": "$"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.id",
                "type": "remove"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.id",
                "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas",
                "type": "remove"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[1]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[2]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.emails[0].primary",
                "condition": "$.emails[0].length() > 0",
                "constant": true
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.meta",
                "type": "remove"
            },
            {
                "constant": "userName",
                "targetVariable": "entityCorrelationAttributeName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.userName",
                "targetVariable": "entityCorrelationAttributeValue"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:PatchOp",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.meta",
                "type": "remove",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[0].op",
                "condition": "$.nickName EMPTY false",
                "constant": "add",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[0].path",
                "condition": "$.nickName EMPTY false",
                "constant": "nickName",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.nickName",
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[0].value",
                "condition": "$.nickName EMPTY false",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].op",
                "constant": "add",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            }
        ]
    }
}

 

 

The problem occurs when we want to update the group

following code is added to the transformation 

 

 

            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].op",
                "constant": "add",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].path",
                "constant": "groups",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].value",
                "constant": "xxxxxx-7f28-4b83-9aaf-xxxxxx",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            }

 

 

And error is generated

Question, how to prepare correct transformation for user group update?

A.

entry.title: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The following problem:

As far as I know, the UPN is typically set as an email address.

In my scenario, users are provisioned from Azure -> IAS -> cloud applications.

However, when provisioning users from IAS  (for example) to Enable Now, the usernames are created as email addresses.

 

 

How can I avoid this?

For example, in Entra ID Source System, the User Name is defined in the Default Code as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
},

So, this means that the UPN (Azure) becomes the Login Name ( in IAS).

For example, if I map the employee ID to the username in the IPS transformation, the user is created in Enable Now with the employee ID (which would be ideal for us) as the username, but the users cannot authenticate in Azure with the employee ID.

Is there a way to change the UPN?

Or is there another solution to ensure that the usernames in cloud applications are not created as email addresses, while users can still authenticate in Azure?

Thank you very much !!!

Best regards

entry.title: Can't find Active Directory system under Target system of IPS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I got two CIS tenants, one with "trail" and other one as "additional tenant" and for some reason I can't find Active Directory or MS Entra ID or LDAP as Target as given in help document.

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/target-microsoft-active-directory

However, I can see these systems as my source, but I want these systems as my target.

Not sure if I'm missing any configuration, or is it because of the CIS tenant type I'm subscribed to?

-RP

entry.title: IPS transformation assignGroup and multiple groups assignment
entry.content:
Hi All

We are trying to assign groups to users via transformation. It works if no conditions are used. But  if we have more than one condition, only one group is assigned. SCIM 2.0 is used.

We have following gorups

on IAS

On Onprem 

Z_ABAP_GROUP_DEV,

Z_ABAP_GROUP_TST

User has both groups assigned on ABAP system. 

On source system transformation we have 

 

 

{
"targetPath": "$.groups[0].value",
"condition": "$.ACTIVITYGROUPS[?(@.AGR_NAME == 'Z_ABAP_GROUP_TST)] EMPTY false",
"constant": "xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84",
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.groups[1].value",
"condition": "$.ACTIVITYGROUPS[?(@.AGR_NAME == 'Z_ABAP_GROUP_DEV')] EMPTY false",
"constant": "xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374",
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
}

 

 

And this works.

Payload is send to the Target transformation, both groups included

 

on Target side we have 

 

 

{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84"
}
],
"targetVariable": "assignGroup"
},
{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374"
}
],
"targetVariable": "assignGroup"
}

 

 

In result only one group (with id xxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374 - second form transformation - TST)  is assigned to the user 

If user has only one OnPrem group assigned (...DEV with id xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84 first in transformation) this group is assigned properly. 

 

 

Any idea how to fix this ? (Ticket to SAP raised) 

Following code if first "targetVariable": "assignGroup" is removed doesn't works as well 

 

 

{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84"
}
]
},
{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374"
}
],
"targetVariable": "assignGroup"
}

 

 

Regards

Artur

entry.title: Unable to Upload Meta-data button is disabled please unable this button
entry.content: I am unable to upload Service providor Meta data for SAML 2.0 Configuration

entry.title: LMS-IAS integration without any Learning-only users
entry.content:
Dear all,

I’m wondering if activating the IAS integration with LMS is necessary. From my understanding, this integration is required for learning-only users since the native login page has been removed. If my customer does not have any learning-only users and all users are from the SuccessFactors platform, I believe the IAS integration might be optional. If IAS is not integrated, are there any side effects? I’m concerned because I noticed the checklist in the 'Learning Home Page Readiness Information' under 'Integrated Learning Setting' says that 'SAP Cloud Identity Services Identity Authentication is activated.

 

 

[ Related Information ]
  • User Native Learning Sign-In is Deleted 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/learning-native-login-deletion-2h-2024-innovation-alert/ba-p/144854
  • SAP SuccessFactors Learning and SAP Cloud Identity Services Integration Overview | SAP Help Portal

Thanks

entry.title: Identity Provisioning not creating users after a read/sync no errors
entry.content:
So I'm trying to replicate SAP ECC Business Suite users to SAP Workzone Standard on BTP and have walked through a number of blogs and SAP Help documents to get everything setup.  When I run the job on the SAP Cloud Identity Services provisioning ECC source I see the users coming over and getting processed.  The results are mostly updated/created/read with a few errors which are expected, given the source is a development system with missing emails and other data for some users.

Now, the issue is that NO USERS/GROUPS are created in my custom identity provider as expected and there are no errors or logs that would help explain why they weren’t.  Without any specific errors I have no thread to pull on to try and get down to the root cause.

I’ve included some configuration screenshots for reference, which was gathered from the SAP Help documentation.  Transformations were left untouched which I believe is an important point.

Finally, for simplicity I have added a filter to bring in a single user from the source so that tests can be more quickly iterated; the results are below.

 


Source Configuration:

entry.title: 'Internal error; contact system administrator' : send email to new users on Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
We import new user on CPI application with csv and then click on SEND to send the users initial onboarding email.

For past few days when we click on SEND, below error is received.

There are no changes done on SMTP settings or application.

There are no changes done to email template as well.

The 'RESET email' works and user receive email from Cloud Identity Services.

Same error is observed while setting initial password for a customer.

entry.title: IAS with 2 source systems: SF for users and Entra for groups - possible ?
entry.content:
Hi partner consultants,

Client is looking at having 2 source systems in IPS. Main purpose is they want Groups to come from Azure/Entra and users to be automatically assigned to the groups in IAS and eventually this groups will be used to assign role collections in BTP. Question is if it is feasible (that you have these 2 sources?)?

- Users will come from SF (Typical SuccessFactors IPS Source to IAS IPS Target)

- Groups will come from Azure/Entra (Azure IPS Source to IAS IPS Target)

Standalone, these two will work but together, little bit skeptical. Is there a KBA that could tell exactly this set-up/combination is not a good idea? Also thinking about shared IAS environments that was common in the past and how they worked but appreciate experience and inputs please.

Thank you so much!

@ChrisPaine

entry.title: Google Auth remove account linkage
entry.content:
May I know how to remove the google auth linkage for one account. Since we found that there is no option to remove it when we login as admin to IAS. Can you kindly provide steps to remove the google auth linkage in IAS.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Unable to Sync Users from SuccessFactors to SAC due to unfulfilled condition
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am struggling with syncing users from Source System Successfactors to SAC target system.

When trying to sync users all are skipped due to following reason:

skip reason=Entity condition [(($['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User']['perPersonUuid'] EMPTY false) || ($['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sfsf:2.0:User']['personGUID'] EMPTY false)) &&(($['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sap.ips.sfsf.sac:2.0:User']['hasEmbeddedAnalyticsPermission'] empty true) || ($['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sap.ips.sfsf.sac:2.0:User']['hasEmbeddedAnalyticsPermission'] == true))] is not fulfilled

I checked the permission for some users and Reporting Permission is enabled.

I found SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003486565 stating the same error and followed the resolution steps. Unfortunately this did not solve the problem. Users keep getting skipped. I am afraid that it will not work because we reset both our source (SF System) and SAC target system before running the sync.

 

We are using SCIM with sf.api.version=2

ips.delete.existedbefore.entities is set to true in target.

Transformations are set according to implementation guide.

 

Can anyone help with a solution to get this fixed?

 

Thanks

Johannes

entry.title: how can we change global uuid to customer value in IAS user store
entry.content:
We are trying to use our own customer ID for the global uuid within the IAS user record in the Identity Directory as described in this SAP doc: Global User ID in Integration Scenarios | SAP Help Portal. 

In our solution, users are provisioned via IPS from SuccessFactors and we want this to include a customer value for the global uuid, but the updates we have made to the transformations for the IPS SCIM API read of SuccessFactors as source (IAS as target) as described in the document have not worked.

Has anyone successfully deployed their own customer value into global uuid in IAS in this way who has any insight?

entry.title: MFA in IAS Admin Console
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are looking to enable MFA for IAS Admin Console so that the administrators can login via a secure mechanism & not be entering password manually.

Understand we can configure MFA at the application level ( under Applications & Resources) & choose the Admin console->Risk Based Authentication & then enable Two factor authentication. 

Can you please let us know if this is the standard way to enable MFA at application level or is there any other best practices to be followed .

Please help to share.

Thanks
Regards

Vidya

entry.title: Error in group provisioning to commerce cloud
entry.content:
Hello All,

Error occurring in Commerce cloud group provisioning .

User able to create bit fails in group provisioning.

Please suggest

Thanks

entry.title: IAS application:Subject Name Identifier
entry.content:
Hi All,

Please suggest the meaning of Source in IAS for an application, in 'Subject Name Identifier' .

regards

Plaban

entry.title: IAS applications: SAML 2.0
entry.content:
Hi All,

For IAS applications, Is SAML 2.0 planned to be deprecated

Regards Plaban
